featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Run a complete feature table filtering pipeline with QC report

    run_filtering_pipeline.Rd

    A comprehensive pipeline that loads, filters, and analyzes a feature table. Allows selection of different coverage and abundance filtering methods. Produces QC metrics and visualizations comparing before/after filtering.

    Generates the following outputs: filtered feature table (TSV format), sample coverage distribution plot, feature abundance distribution plot, sample sparsity histogram (per-sample view), retention rates barplot, heatmaps of top variable features (original and filtered), top features stacked barplot, presence frequency comparison plot (side-by-side histograms), text summary report.

    run_filtering_pipeline(input_file,
                        output_dir = ".",
                        prefix = "filtered",
                        # Coverage filtering
                        cov_filter_method = c("none", "absolute", "mad", "iqr"),
                        cov_threshold = NULL,
                        cov_floor = 0,
                        # Abundance filtering
                        abun_filter_method = c("none", "absolute", "relative", "relative_cutoff"),
                        abun_threshold = NULL,
                        abun_min_samples = 1,
                        # Additional options
                        min_coverage_for_relative = 1000,
                        remove_features = TRUE,
                        generate_plots = TRUE,
                        generate_report = TRUE,
                        verbose = TRUE)

    Arguments

    input_file

    Path to input feature table file (TSV or CSV).

    output_dir

    Directory to save outputs (filtered table, plots, report). Default is current directory.

    prefix

    Prefix for output filenames. Default is "filtered".

    cov_filter_method

    Method for sample coverage filtering:

    "none"

    No coverage filtering

    "absolute"

    Use fixed min_reads threshold

    "mad"

    Use MAD-based threshold estimation (median +/- multiplier * MAD)

    "iqr"

    Use IQR/Tukey-based threshold estimation (fences)

    cov_threshold

    Fixed threshold if method="absolute", or multiplier if method="mad"/"iqr". For MAD/IQR, this multiplies the spread statistic.

    cov_floor

    Minimum possible coverage cutoff when using MAD/IQR methods.

    abun_filter_method

    Method for feature abundance filtering:

    "none"

    No abundance filtering

    "absolute"

    Filter features below absolute count threshold

    "relative"

    Filter features below relative abundance threshold

    "relative_cutoff"

    Use relative threshold based on min-coverage sample

    abun_threshold

    Threshold value for abundance filtering. For "absolute": minimum read count per feature. For "relative": proportion (e.g., 0.01 for 1%). For "relative_cutoff": relative proportion used to calculate absolute threshold.

    abun_min_samples

    Minimum number of samples where feature must exceed threshold (default: 1).

    min_coverage_for_relative

    If using "relative_cutoff" method, minimum coverage for samples.

    remove_features

    Logical. Remove features below threshold? Default TRUE.

    generate_plots

    Logical. Generate QC visualization plots? Default TRUE.

    generate_report

    Logical. Generate text summary report? Default TRUE.

    verbose

    Logical. Print progress messages? Default TRUE.

    Value

    A list containing:

    original_table

    Original loaded table

    filtered_table

    Filtered table

    qc_metrics

    List of QC metrics from compute_filtering_qc()

    presence_stats

    List of presence analysis statistics (including comparison data)

    filtering_summary

    Summary of filtering steps applied

    Examples

    # Simple pipeline with absolute thresholds
# result <- run_filtering_pipeline(
#   input_file = "feature-table.tsv",
#   output_dir = "results",
#   prefix = "analysis1",
#   cov_filter_method = "absolute",
#   cov_threshold = 1000,
#   abun_filter_method = "absolute",
#   abun_threshold = 5
# )

# Pipeline with MAD-based coverage and relative abundance filtering
# result <- run_filtering_pipeline(
#   input_file = "feature-table.tsv",
#   output_dir = "results",
#   cov_filter_method = "mad",
#   cov_threshold = 3,
#   abun_filter_method = "relative_cutoff",
#   abun_threshold = 0.01,
#   min_coverage_for_relative = 1000
# )
